campaign: GPU wedge ceiling probes — 3 cheap-first oracles
parent: BC3 decomp PR #1697 (epilogue-fusion ceiling 1.085× too small)
host: ubu-2 RTX 5070 sm_120 (driver 580, cuBLAS 12, FP32 peak 31.2 TFLOPS)

small_m_sub_roofline.finding: cuBLAS SGEMM at M=1 leaves 99.05% of FP32 peak unused (0.295 of 31.2 TFLOPS). M=8 leaves 92.5%. The decode-batch regime (M ≤ 64) is severely sub-roofline. A hand-emit GEMV-tiled kernel could reclaim multiples of this — REAL >>1.5× ceiling.
small_m_sub_roofline.implication: Single-token LLM decode (M=1) is the canonical hexa wedge. vLLM/llama.cpp ship custom CUDA GEMV kernels specifically because cuBLAS underperforms here. Hexa hand-emit GEMV (warp-level reduce + bcast) could beat cuBLAS 5-10× at M=1, 2-3× at M=8.
small_m_sub_roofline.next_round_target: F-WEDGE-SMALL-M-GEMV-WALL — hexa-native GEMV kernel timed wall vs cuBLAS at M ∈ {1, 8, 32}

grouped_qkv.result_tier: ORANGE — INSUFFICIENT (CUDA illegal memory access in cublasSgemmStridedBatched call at line 118)
grouped_qkv.finding: stride-batched API errored before producing measurements. Stride layout suspect (column-major leading-dim mixing with batched offsets — needs revisit). Not critical given small-M + top-K findings provide stronger wedges; deferred to follow-up.

topk_fusion_ceiling.finding: Top-K (full sort) costs 40-44% of total at M=8-32 vocab projection — fusion ceiling 1.68-1.80×. Even with optimal radix-select top-K (cub::DeviceSegmentedRadixSort) the share would remain non-trivial because the radix passes still scan the M·N output.
topk_fusion_ceiling.caveat: thrust::sort is O(N log N); a production NN stack would use cub::DeviceSegmentedRadixSort O(N) per row. Optimal top-K share would be 10-25% (estimate), so the realistic ceiling is more like 1.10-1.30× — still above BC3 epilogue 1.085×.
topk_fusion_ceiling.implication: §5j top-k+GEMM fusion is a real wedge at LM-head shapes, especially with vocab > 100K (Qwen-class). Hand-emit GEMM + warp-level top-K accumulator (streaming top-K computed during GEMM output reduce) could compete here.
topk_fusion_ceiling.next_round_target: F-WEDGE-TOPK-FUSED-WALL — hexa-native GEMM-with-streaming-top-K kernel vs cublasSgemm + cub::DeviceSegmentedRadixSort

methodology: feedback_instrument_first_methodology — cheap-first oracle (3 launchers, ~5min total fire) quantified 3 wedges' ceilings BEFORE any heavy codegen campaign. Two surfaced real >1.5× targets (small-M GEMV + top-K fusion); BC3 epilogue retired as roofline-bounded.
